This enterprise, named 'Jiarui Huizhi Optoelectronic Technology,' was rigidly stuffed into the broad category of 'Electrical Machinery Manufacturing' by the authorities. Its lengthy business license speaks of 'R&D of optoelectronic and lighting electrical products' in the first half, but drastically pivots in the second half to: 'various engineering construction, earthwork engineering, interior decoration, landscaping.' The owner rents an office on the 4th floor and maintains a dozen project managers, designers, and salespeople. They bid on projects everywhere (such as municipal lighting, building facade lighting, residential landscaping, etc.), and upon winning, they purchase lighting fixtures (or even hire someone to design custom appearances), and then bring the earthwork and construction crews to the site to work. The official 'Manufacturing' tag has been subjected to the most thorough cross-boundary stripping by the system.
